whatThisIs: Check whether a qualifying life event opens an ACA Special Enrollment Period on the Marketplace. The ACA's SEP rules at 45 CFR 155.420 list the qualifying events - loss of minimum essential coverage, marriage, birth or adoption of a child, permanent move to a new coverage area, gain of citizenship or lawful presence, and a short list of exceptional circumstances - and each event has its own 60-day window (sometimes 60 days before and 60 days after, sometimes only after the event). Sophie Well asks for the event type and date and returns whether the window is open today.
whenToUse: Use this when someone outside the annual open enrollment period needs to know whether they can still buy a Marketplace plan, when a job loss or a move triggers questions about coverage gaps, or when an attestation form asks for the SEP basis and you need the regulatory category name. SEP determinations get verified by the Marketplace later, so picking the right qualifying-event label up front is the difference between an effective date that lines up cleanly and a coverage gap that opens because the application was filed under the wrong category.
